commands.review_summary:
The branch adds a cooperative scheduler to the Ollama native NDJSON stream loop, preserves abort classification during dense stream drains, and adds regression tests for timer progress and abort behavior.

PR surface: Source +47, Tests +86. Total +133 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. source-reproducible rather than live-reproduced here: current main drains parsed Ollama NDJSON chunks without a cooperative yield in the stream loop, and the linked issue supplies event-loop starvation logs. I did not run the Windows/live Ollama scenario in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - No automated repair is indicated; maintainer handling is needed for the protected maintainer PR, current-main refresh, and final live-provider proof judgment.

target.title: fix(ollama): yield during dense stream processing
target.body:
## Summary

- rebase the Ollama dense stream cooperative-yield fix onto a current maintainer branch, preserving @udaymanish6 as commit author
- yield and check aborts while draining dense native Ollama NDJSON chunks so one large read cannot monopolize the Gateway event loop
- preserve caller cancellation semantics during dense stream processing and add regression coverage for timer progress plus abort classification

Refs https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/86599.
Supersedes https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/86633 as the rebased maintainer branch.

## Verification

- `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD` - passed on final head `e00fa780a23`.
- `node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check --threads=1 extensions/ollama/src/stream.ts extensions/ollama/src/stream.test.ts` - passed on final head `e00fa780a23`.
- `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs extensions/ollama/src/stream.ts extensions/ollama/src/stream.test.ts` - passed on final head `e00fa780a23`.
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/ollama/src/stream.test.ts --reporter=dot` - 1 file / 12 tests passed on final head `e00fa780a23`.
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch --base origin/main` - clean after the timer-phase scheduler fix; no accepted/actionable findings; `overall: patch is correct (0.86)`.
- AWS Crabbox changed gate after the timer-phase scheduler fix: `node scripts/crabbox-wrapper.mjs run --provider aws --target linux --idle-timeout 90m --ttl 180m --timing-json --shell -- "pnpm check:changed"`; provider `aws`, lease `cbx_c4c8ac13ee86`, slug `swift-shrimp`, run `run_8fd2ad9a4885`, machine `c7a.8xlarge`, exit `0`, `leaseStopped=true`, lanes `extensions`, `extensionTests`.
- Final rebase over `ef9e9bf6b92` was clean; the focused final-head diff/format/lint/test proof above was rerun after that rebase.
- Local Ollama live check was not available in this environment: `ollama` CLI unavailable and `http://127.0.0.1:11434/api/tags` not reachable.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: dense native Ollama stream chunks now cooperatively yield during parsing instead of draining an entire large read in one synchronous loop, while caller aborts during the drain surface as `aborted` rather than provider `error`.
Real environment tested: focused local Vitest wrapper in a linked worktree, formatting/lint checks, branch autoreview, and AWS Crabbox Linux changed gate.
Exact steps or command run after this patch: See the Verification section above.
Evidence after fix: refreshed head `e00fa780a23`; focused stream tests passed with the dense-yield and abort regressions; AWS Crabbox `pnpm check:changed` passed the extension and extension test lanes after the timer-phase scheduler fix.
Observed result after fix: dense stream unit coverage confirms timer progress during parser drain, aborts scheduled before the first cooperative yield are observed before the stream can finish, aborts during dense processing keep `reason` and assistant `stopReason` set to `aborted`, and changed extension type/lint/import-cycle guards passed remotely.
What was not tested: true live Ollama runtime was not available in this local environment; Windows local-daemon reproduction from https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/86599 was not rerun in this PR. This is one mitigation in the broader event-loop starvation cluster, not the final issue closeout by itself.
